message.content:
You are verifier #1, independent. Do NOT optimistically confirm. Read the ACTUAL code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay and independently determine whether this is a TRUE production-readiness problem in the CURRENT code (3 hardening passes already applied).

Finding G-021: build_baseline_artifact lets hash_value raise raw ValueError/TypeError on non-finite or unhashable extra_inputs
File: src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py:140
Description: At determinism.py:140 input_hashes[name] = hash_value(value) is called for every extra_input. hash_value -> canonical_json uses allow_nan=False and canonical_plain refuses non-JSON-native leaves, so a NaN/Inf float raises a bare ValueError ('Out of range float values are not JSON compliant') and an unhashable leaf raises a bare TypeError. preregistration.canonical_hypothesis_digest (preregistration.py:120-127) deliberately catches these and re-raises a typed, contextual PreRegistrationError naming the offending input; build_baseline_artifact — the other public consumer of the same canonical hashing — does not, so the determinism harness surfaces a low-context raw exception that does not identify which extra_input was bad.
PoC: build_baseline_artifact(corpus, {}, extra_inputs={'cfg': {'thr': float('nan')}}) -> ValueError: Out of range float values are not JSON compliant (no indication extra_input 'cfg' is the culprit).
Suggested fix: Wrap per-input hashing: for name, value in (extra_inputs or {}).items(): try: input_hashes[name] = hash_value(value) except (ValueError, TypeError) as exc: raise ValueError(f"extra_input {name!r} cannot be canonicalized for a reproducible hash ({exc}); use JSON-native/finite values") from None.

Confirmed=true ONLY if the problem exists as described in current code, the PoC is reproducible, and it is a genuine concern (not speculative, not already-handled, not intended design). Be concrete and cite file:line.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

100	    corpus: Corpus,
101	    contents: Mapping[str, Any],
102	    *,
103	    component_versions: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
104	    extra_inputs: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
105	    seed: int | None = None,
106	    corpus_hash: str | None = None,
107	    trust_corpus_hash: bool = False,
108	) -> BaselineArtifact:
109	    """Assemble a :class:`BaselineArtifact` with a complete determinism record.
110	
111	    ``contents`` is the builder's output (embeddings, similarity graph, clusters, …).
112	    ``component_versions`` records the builder + any model/library versions (the engine and
113	    Python versions are added automatically). ``extra_inputs`` are additional inputs to hash
114	    (e.g. config). ``seed``, if omitted, is derived deterministically from the corpus + inputs
115	    so it is reproducible.
116	
117	    ``corpus_hash`` lets a caller pass an already-computed :func:`corpus_fingerprint` (#119). By
118	    default it is **verified** against the corpus and a mismatch raises ``ValueError`` — a
119	    stale/typo'd hash would otherwise produce a determinism record whose fingerprint and derived
120	    seed describe a *different* corpus than the one passed, silently defeating the reproducibility
121	    guarantee this harness exists to provide (#155). Pass ``trust_corpus_hash=True`` to skip the
122	    recompute (the #119 optimization) only when the caller computed the hash from this exact
123	    corpus and accepts integrity responsibility.
124	    """
125	    if corpus_hash is None:
126	        corpus_hash = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
127	    elif not trust_corpus_hash:
128	        # Verify the supplied hash binds the corpus actually passed (default-safe, #155).
129	        actual = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
130	        if corpus_hash != actual:
131	            raise ValueError(
132	                f"corpus_hash {corpus_hash!r} does not match the corpus "
133	                f"(corpus_fingerprint={actual!r}) — the determinism record would bind the wrong "
134	                "data; pass trust_corpus_hash=True only if you accept integrity responsibility"
135	            )
136	    if extra_inputs and "corpus" in extra_inputs:
137	        raise ValueError("'corpus' is a reserved input-hash key — rename the extra input (#B7)")
138	    input_hashes = {"corpus": corpus_hash}
139	    for name, value in (extra_inputs or {}).items():
140	        input_hashes[name] = hash_value(value)
141	
142	    versions = {"engine": _ENGINE_VERSION, "python": sys.version.split()[0]}
143	    versions.update(component_versions or {})
144	
145	    if seed is None:
146	        # Derive from all input hashes (the corpus hash is already among them under "corpus").
147	        seed = stable_seed(*(input_hashes[k] for k in sorted(input_hashes)))
148	
149	    record = DeterminismRecord(seed=seed, input_hashes=input_hashes, component_versions=versions)
150	    return BaselineArtifact(
151	        corpus_fingerprint=corpus_hash, contents=contents, determinism=record.as_dict()
152	    )

/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/methodology/preregistration.py
/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py
---
/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/_canonical.py:32:def canonical_plain(value: Any) -> Any:
/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/_canonical.py:68:def canonical_json(value: Any) -> str:
/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/_canonical.py:81:def hash_value(value: Any) -> str:

1	"""Type-faithful canonical hashing — the engine's single content-digest primitive.
2	
3	Two unrelated places need a *stable, type-faithful* content hash: the baseline determinism
4	harness (so the same inputs reproduce the same baseline) and pre-registration (so a lock
5	binds the exact hypothesis content). Both must distinguish values that merely share a
6	``str()`` — ``int 1`` from ``str "1"``, ``date(2020,1,1)`` from ``"2020-01-01"`` — or the
7	hash is a liability rather than a guarantee.
8	
9	This module is the one implementation, hoisted here so it sits *below* both
10	``methodology`` and ``baseline`` in the import graph (neither may depend on the other). It is
11	dependency-free and pure. Anything it cannot canonicalize reproducibly is refused **loudly**:
12	a default (address-based) ``repr`` would make the digest differ every process and silently
13	break the guarantee it exists to provide.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import datetime as _dt
19	import decimal
20	import hashlib
21	import json
22	import uuid
23	from typing import Any, Mapping
24	
25	
26	def keytag(key: Any) -> str:
27	    """A type-faithful, sortable string for a mapping key, so distinct keys that happen to
28	    share a ``str()`` (e.g. int ``1`` vs str ``"1"``) do NOT collide."""
29	    return f"{type(key).__name__}::{key!r}"
30	
31	
32	def canonical_plain(value: Any) -> Any:
33	    """Canonicalize ``value`` into a fully JSON-native, type-faithful structure for hashing.
34	
35	    Mappings become a sorted list of ``[typed-key, value]`` pairs; bytes, sets, and any
36	    non-JSON-native leaf are tagged with their type rather than stringified. Nothing is coerced
37	    with ``str()``, so the content hash distinguishes values that share a ``str()``.
38	    """
39	    if value is None or isinstance(value, (str, bool, int, float)):
40	        return value  # JSON-native scalars; json encodes 1, "1", true, 1.0 distinctly
41	    if isinstance(value, bytes):
42	        return {"__bytes__": value.hex()}
43	    if isinstance(value, Mapping):
44	        pairs = sorted(
45	            ([keytag(k), canonical_plain(v)] for k, v in value.items()), key=lambda p: p[0]
46	        )
47	        return {"__map__": pairs}
48	    if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
49	        return [canonical_plain(v) for v in value]
50	    if isinstance(value, (set, frozenset)):
51	        return {"__set__": sorted((canonical_plain(v) for v in value), key=repr)}
52	    # A small allowlist of leaf types with a value-faithful canonical form.
53	    if isinstance(value, (_dt.date, _dt.datetime, _dt.time)):
54	        return {"__temporal__": value.isoformat()}
55	    if isinstance(value, decimal.Decimal):
56	        return {"__decimal__": str(value)}
57	    if isinstance(value, uuid.UUID):
58	        return {"__uuid__": str(value)}
59	    # Anything else is refused LOUDLY rather than repr-tagged: a default (address-based) repr
60	    # would make the digest differ every process, silently breaking the guarantee.
61	    raise TypeError(
62	        f"cannot canonicalize a leaf of type "
63	        f"{type(value).__module__}.{type(value).__qualname__!r} reproducibly — convert it to a "
64	        "JSON-native value, bytes, date/datetime/Decimal/UUID before hashing"
65	    )
66	
67	
68	def canonical_json(value: Any) -> str:
69	    """The canonical JSON text of ``value`` — stable across processes and key orderings."""
70	    return json.dumps(canonical_plain(value), sort_keys=True, allow_nan=False)
71	
72	
73	def hash_bytes(data: bytes) -> str:
74	    return hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest()
75	
76	
77	def hash_text(text: str) -> str:
78	    return hash_bytes(text.encode("utf-8"))
79	
80	
81	def hash_value(value: Any) -> str:
82	    """Stable, type-faithful content hash of an arbitrary value.
83	
84	    ``canonical_plain`` canonicalizes everything to JSON-native form first, so ``json.dumps``
85	    needs no ``default=`` coercion — anything it still cannot encode is a real bug to surface,
86	    not to silently stringify.
87	    """
88	    return hash_text(canonical_json(value))
89

content.text:
Confirmed. `build_baseline_artifact` (line 99) calls `hash_value(value)` at line 140 inside a plain loop with no try/except. Both error cases reproduce raw (ValueError for NaN, TypeError for unhashable leaf), neither identifying which `extra_input` is the culprit.

The PoC is accurate. The asymmetry with `canonical_hypothesis_digest` (preregistration.py:120-127) is real and intentional there but absent here.

Assessment: This is a real but minor diagnostics-quality gap. The error IS raised loudly (no silent corruption, no incorrect output), and the exception messages do contain content ("nan", the type name). The only deficiency is that the offending `extra_input` name isn't surfaced — a debuggability concern, not a correctness, safety, or data-integrity defect. It is a genuine inconsistency given preregistration deliberately wraps the same primitive, so it's a defensible polish item, but the severity is low.

input.reasoning:
Verified against current code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay. build_baseline_artifact (determinism.py:99) loops over extra_inputs at lines 139-140 calling input_hashes[name] = hash_value(value) with no per-input try/except. hash_value -> canonical_json (_canonical.py:70) uses allow_nan=False, and canonical_plain (_canonical.py:39-65) refuses non-JSON-native leaves with a bare TypeError. I reproduced both cases by loading _canonical.py standalone: {'thr': float('nan')} -> "ValueError: Out of range float values are not JSON compliant: nan"; {'x': object()} -> "TypeError: cannot canonicalize a leaf of type builtins.'object' reproducibly". Neither message names the offending extra_input key. The asymmetry the finding cites is real: canonical_hypothesis_digest (preregistration.py:120-127) deliberately wraps the identical primitive in try/except (ValueError, TypeError) and re-raises a typed PreRegistrationError naming the hypothesis, while build_baseline_artifact does not. So the PoC is reproducible and the inconsistency is genuine.

However, the severity is low. The failure mode is a loud raise, not silent corruption or a wrong determinism record — the harness fails closed. The raw messages do carry content ("nan", the leaf type), so they are not opaque; the only missing detail is the extra_input *name*. This is a debuggability/consistency polish item, not a correctness, safety, or reproducibility defect. The suggested fix (wrap the per-input loop and name the key) is reasonable and matches the established preregistration pattern.

content.text: Confirmed G-021 as a true but low-severity finding. The asymmetry is real: `build_baseline_artifact` at `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py:139-140` calls `hash_value(value)` per extra_input with no wrapping, while the parallel consumer `canonical_hypothesis_digest` at `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/methodology/preregistration.py:120-127` deliberately catches `(ValueError, TypeError)` and re-raises a typed, named error. I reproduced both raw exceptions. Severity is low because the harness fails loudly (no silent corruption); the only gap is that the offending extra_input key isn't named in the message.
